---
title: "Command Architecture Roadmap"
---

## Progress Summary

*Last updated: 2026-03-11*

| Phase | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| **Phase 1:** Command Foundation | Complete | Steps 1.1–1.14 complete. GUI audit (1.10) restructured as on-demand. |
| **Phase 2:** Triage Session | In Progress | Steps 2.1–2.3, 2.6–2.8 complete. Keyboard shortcuts (2.4) and tracked-regions visualization (2.5) remain. |
| **Phase 3:** Persistence Commands & Save Registry | Complete | SaverRegistry infrastructure, 8 savers (CSV ×6, CapnProto, OpenCV), SaveData + LoadData commands, round-trip fuzz tests |
| **Phase 4:** Action Journal Integration | Not Started | Full journal; builds on CommandRecorder from Step 1.13 |
| **Phase 5:** Undo/Redo | Not Started | CommandStack, memento-based EditorState undo, inverse commands |
| **Phase 6:** CLI Batch Executor & Macro Recording | Not Started | Headless command sequence execution; builds on golden trace tests from Step 1.14 |

For the full architectural design, types, and code examples, see the
[Command Architecture Design Document](index.qmd).

## Dependency Graph

```
Phase 1: Command Foundation
   │
   ├── Step 1.13 (CommandRecorder + MutationGuard)
   │       │
   │       ├──► On-demand widget audits (Step 1.10)
   │       │       MutationGuard identifies gaps at runtime
   │       │
   │       └──► Phase 4: Action Journal Integration
   │               CommandRecorder generalizes into full journal
   │
   ├── Step 1.14 (DataManagerSnapshot + Golden Trace Tests)
   │       │
   │       └──► Phase 6: CLI Batch & Macro Recording
   │               Golden traces generalize into macro recording
   │
   ├──► Phase 2: Triage Session
   │       Steps 2.7–2.8 (guided editor) depend on Steps 1.11–1.12
   │
   ├──► Phase 3: Persistence Commands & Save Registry
   │
   └──► Phase 5: Undo/Redo
```

**Parallelizable work:**

- Phases 2, 3, and 5 are independent of each other and can proceed in parallel after Phase 1.
- Steps 1.11–1.12 (ParameterSchema extraction + CommandInfo) are prerequisites for Steps
  2.7–2.8 (AutoParamWidget extraction + guided pipeline editor) but independent of all other
  Phase 2 work.
- Steps 1.13–1.14 (CommandRecorder, MutationGuard, DataManagerSnapshot) are independent of
  Phases 2, 3, and 5 and can proceed in parallel.
- Phase 4 depends on both Step 1.13 (CommandRecorder) and the Action Journal from the State
  Management Roadmap. CommandRecorder is a lightweight precursor that feeds directly into it.
- Phase 6 can begin once Phases 1, 3, and 4 are complete (Phase 5/undo is not required).
  Golden trace tests (Step 1.14) provide the replay infrastructure that Phase 6 generalizes.

---

## Phase 1 — Command Foundation

**Goal:** Minimal, testable core. No Qt dependency.

**Depends on:** Nothing

### Step 1.1 — Core Types

Create `src/DataManager/Commands/` directory and CMake target (`Commands` static library).
Implement the four core structs, all reflect-cpp serializable:

- [x] `CommandResult` — success/error + affected keys
- [x] `CommandContext` — holds `shared_ptr<DataManager>`, runtime variables, optional progress callback
- [x] `CommandDescriptor` — single command invocation (name + parameters + description)
- [x] `CommandSequenceDescriptor` — sequence of commands with optional name, version, and template variables

See [Core Types](index.qmd#core-types) in the design document.

### Step 1.2 — ICommand Base Class

- [x] `ICommand` abstract base with `execute()`, `commandName()`, `toJson()`, `isUndoable()`, `undo()`, `mergeId()`, `mergeWith()`
- [x] Default implementations: `isUndoable()` → false, `undo()` → error, `mergeId()` → -1, `mergeWith()` → false

See [ICommand Base Class](index.qmd#icommand-base-class) in the design document.

### Step 1.3 — Command Factory

- [x] `createCommand()` free function: maps `command_name` string + `rfl::Generic` parameters → `unique_ptr<ICommand>`
- [x] Returns `nullptr` for unknown command names or deserialization failures

See [Command Factory](index.qmd#command-factory) in the design document.

### Step 1.4 — Variable Substitution

- [x] Implement two-pass `${variable}` substitution (static pass from `CommandSequenceDescriptor::variables`, runtime pass from `CommandContext::runtime_variables`)
- [x] Reuse the pattern from TransformsV2 pipeline variable substitution

See [Variable Substitution](index.qmd#variable-substitution) in the design document.

### Step 1.5 — Sequence Execution

- [x] `executeSequence()` free function: iterates descriptors, applies variable substitution, creates + executes commands, collects executed commands in `SequenceResult` for undo
- [x] Stop-on-first-error behavior (configurable)

See [Sequence Execution](index.qmd#sequence-execution) in the design document.

### Step 1.6 — `getEntityIdsInRange` Helpers

- [x] Free function template for `RaggedTimeSeries<T>` types (LineData, PointData, MaskData) using `flattened_data()`
- [x] Overload for `DigitalEventSeries` using `view()`
- [x] Overload for `DigitalIntervalSeries` collecting IDs of overlapping intervals
- [x] These are pure query functions living in `src/DataManager/Commands/`

See [getEntityIdsInRange](index.qmd#getentityidsinrange-free-functions) in the design document.

### Step 1.7 — First Four Commands

Implement concrete command classes with parameter structs:

- [x] `MoveByTimeRange` — moves entities in `[start_frame, end_frame]` between same-type data objects. Captures moved entity IDs for undo.
- [x] `CopyByTimeRange` — copies entities (source unmodified). Wider type support than move.
- [x] `AddInterval` — appends an interval to a `DigitalIntervalSeries`. Creates the series if `create_if_missing` is true.
- [x] `ForEachKey` — meta-command iterating a list of values, binding each to a template variable, and executing sub-commands per item. Undo reverses sub-commands in reverse order.

See [Commands](index.qmd#commands) in the design document.

### Step 1.8 — Unit Tests

- [x] Factory lookup: known command names return non-null; unknown returns null
- [x] Variable substitution: static + runtime variables resolve correctly in parameters
- [x] Command execution: `MoveByTimeRange` moves entities between data objects
- [x] Command execution: `CopyByTimeRange` copies without modifying source
- [x] Command execution: `AddInterval` creates/appends intervals
- [x] Sequence execution: multi-command sequence runs in order, stops on error
- [x] `ForEachKey` expansion: sub-commands execute once per item with correct variable binding
- [x] `getEntityIdsInRange` correctness for each supported data type
- [x] Error handling: missing keys, type mismatches, empty ranges

### Step 1.9 — Validation (Stretch Goal)

- [x] `validateSequence()` function with `ValidationResult` (warnings + errors)
- [x] Structural check: all command names are valid factory keys
- [x] Deserialization check: parameters parse into expected structs
- [x] Variable resolution check: all `${variable}` references resolve
- [x] Data key existence check: warns if referenced keys missing from DataManager
- [x] Type compatibility check: errors if source/destination types don't match

See [Validation](index.qmd#validation) in the design document.

### Step 1.10 — GUI Verb Audit (On-Demand)

This step has its own detailed tracking document: [GUI Verb Audit](gui_verb_audit.qmd).

**Strategy change (2026-03-09):** The GUI audit is no longer a blocking prerequisite that must
be completed exhaustively before other work proceeds. Instead, it uses a hybrid approach:

1. **Quick triage pass** — `grep` for `_data_manager` in each widget's headers to identify
   which widgets have any `DataManager` interaction at all. Widgets with zero references
   move to the excluded list immediately.
2. **Detailed audit on-demand** — When you are about to implement commands for a widget or
   refactor it, perform the full audit (Phase A–D) at that time.
3. **Runtime gap detection** — The MutationGuard (Step 1.13) automatically identifies
   unrecorded `DataManager` mutations at runtime, replacing manual code-reading for
   gap discovery.
4. **Golden trace verification** — A widget is considered "done" when all its mutations
   route through commands and a golden trace test verifies the workflow (Step 1.14).

See the [Definition of Done](#definition-of-done) section for widget completion criteria.

Completed widget audits:

- [x] DataManager_Widget — 3 verbs (2 new commands: `CreateData`, `DeleteData`)
- [x] DataInspector_Widget — ~30 verbs (10 covered, 14 new commands, 6 future Phase 3)

Remaining widgets are audited on-demand as they are touched. See the
[GUI Verb Audit](gui_verb_audit.qmd) document for the quick triage results and per-widget
audit tables.

### Step 1.11 — Extract ParameterSchema Library

- [x] Create `src/ParameterSchema/` as a new static library
- [x] Move `ParameterSchema.hpp/.cpp` out of `src/TransformsV2/core/` into `src/ParameterSchema/`
- [x] Move `ParameterFieldDescriptor`, `ParameterSchema`, `extractParameterSchema<T>()`, `ParameterUIHints<T>`, and helper functions (`snakeCaseToDisplay`, `parseUnderlyingType`, `extractConstraints`)
- [x] Library depends only on reflect-cpp and standard library — no TransformsV2 types
- [x] Update TransformsV2 CMakeLists to depend on `ParameterSchema` and remove moved sources
- [x] Update all TransformsV2 includes to reference the new location
- [x] Verify all existing TransformsV2 tests still pass

See [Command Introspection](index.qmd#command-introspection) in the design document.

### Step 1.12 — CommandInfo and Factory Query Functions

- [x] Define `CommandInfo` struct in `src/DataManager/Commands/CommandInfo.hpp`
- [x] Add `getAvailableCommands()` to `CommandFactory` — returns `std::vector<CommandInfo>` with one entry per command, including `ParameterSchema` generated via `extractParameterSchema<T>()`
- [x] Add `getCommandInfo(name)` to `CommandFactory` — returns `std::optional<CommandInfo>` for a single command
- [x] Add `ParameterUIHints<T>` specializations for command param structs (tooltips, field descriptions)
- [x] Add `ParameterSchema` as a dependency of the `Commands` CMake target
- [x] Unit tests: `getAvailableCommands()` returns all known commands; `getCommandInfo()` returns correct schemas; schema field counts match param struct fields

### Step 1.13 — CommandRecorder and MutationGuard

These are lightweight infrastructure pieces that enable test-driven widget refactoring
without requiring the full Action Journal (Phase 4).

#### CommandRecorder (Phase 4 Lite)

- [x] `CommandRecorder` class in `src/DataManager/Commands/`
- [x] `record(CommandDescriptor)` — appends a descriptor to the trace
- [x] `trace()` — returns the recorded descriptors
- [x] `toSequence()` — produces a `CommandSequenceDescriptor` from the trace
- [x] `clear()` — resets the recorder
- [x] Optional hook in `executeSequence()` — accepts `CommandRecorder*` parameter; records
  each successfully executed command automatically

```cpp
class CommandRecorder {
    std::vector<CommandDescriptor> _trace;
public:
    void record(CommandDescriptor desc) { _trace.push_back(std::move(desc)); }
    [[nodiscard]] auto const& trace() const { return _trace; }
    [[nodiscard]] CommandSequenceDescriptor toSequence(std::string name = "") const;
    void clear() { _trace.clear(); }
};
```

This is intentionally minimal — ~50 lines of code. It feeds directly into Phase 4 (Action
Journal) as the recording mechanism, but is useful immediately for golden trace tests.

#### MutationGuard (Strangulation Pattern)

- [x] Debug-only `thread_local bool g_inside_command` flag in `src/DataManager/Commands/`
- [x] `CommandGuard` RAII struct that sets the flag to `true` during `ICommand::execute()`
- [x] Instrumentation points in `DataManager` mutating methods (`setData()`, `deleteData()`,
  etc.) — `qWarning()` when `g_inside_command` is false
- [x] Compile-gated to debug builds only (`#ifndef NDEBUG`)

This gives **automated runtime gap detection**: run the app normally, exercise each widget,
and the console reports exactly which `DataManager` mutations are not routed through commands.
No manual code reading required.

#### Implementation Note: rfl::Generic Integer Round-Trip

`rfl::Generic` stores all JSON numbers as `double`. When a `rfl::Generic` containing integer
values goes through a `write → read<rfl::Generic>` round-trip, integers like `10` become
`double(10.0)`. When this is subsequently serialized to JSON, the output is `10.0`, which
fails to deserialize into `int64_t` fields via `rfl::json::read<T>()`.

**Fix applied (2026-03-10):**

1. **`normalizeJsonNumbers()`** (`VariableSubstitution.hpp/cpp`): Scans a JSON string and
   converts whole-number floating-point values (e.g., `10.0`, `-5.00`) to integers
   (`10`, `-5`), while leaving fractional values (`10.5`), strings, and exponent notation
   untouched.

2. **`createCommandFromJson()`** (`CommandFactory.hpp/cpp`): New overload that accepts a
   pre-built JSON parameter string. Applies `normalizeJsonNumbers()` before deserialization.
   The original `createCommand(name, rfl::Generic)` now delegates to this function.

3. **`executeSequence()` optimization**: After variable substitution, the substituted JSON
   string is passed directly to `createCommandFromJson()`, skipping the unnecessary
   `rfl::Generic → JSON → rfl::Generic` round-trip that previously caused the integer loss.

This means both literal integers in `rfl::Generic` parameters **and** `${variable}`
substitution work correctly. Tests verify both paths.

### Step 1.14 — DataManagerSnapshot and Golden Trace Tests

These provide the test infrastructure for verifying widget command coverage.

#### DataManagerSnapshot

- [x] `DataManagerSnapshot` struct in `src/DataManager/`
- [x] `snapshotDataManager(DataManager&)` — produces a lightweight summary:
  - `key_type`: map of data key → type name string
  - `key_entity_count`: map of data key → number of entities
  - `key_content_hash`: map of data key → deterministic FNV-1a content hash
- [x] `operator==` for snapshot comparison in tests (defaulted)
- [x] Does not require full data serialization — entity counts and content hashes detect
  regressions; full serialization comes with Phase 3 (SaveData/LoadData)

```cpp
struct DataManagerSnapshot {
    std::map<std::string, std::string> key_type;
    std::map<std::string, size_t> key_entity_count;
    std::map<std::string, std::string> key_content_hash;
};
DataManagerSnapshot snapshotDataManager(DataManager const& dm);
bool operator==(DataManagerSnapshot const&, DataManagerSnapshot const&);
```

#### Golden Trace Test Pattern

- [x] Load a recorded `CommandSequenceDescriptor` from inline JSON
- [x] Replay against a synthesized `DataManager`
- [x] Assert final state via `DataManagerSnapshot` comparison
- [x] Golden trace tests for: CopyByTimeRange, MoveByTimeRange, AddInterval,
  multi-command variable substitution, replay determinism

Example:

```cpp
TEST_CASE("DataManager_Widget CreateData+DeleteData round-trip") {
    auto dm = std::make_shared<DataManager>();
    auto sequence = loadSequenceFromFile("traces/datamanager_widget_create_delete.json");

    CommandContext ctx;
    ctx.data_manager = dm;
    auto result = executeSequence(sequence, ctx);
    REQUIRE(result.success);

    REQUIRE(dm->getKeys().size() == 1);
    REQUIRE(dm->getData<PointData>("my_points") != nullptr);
}
```

This is the TDD mechanism for widget refactoring: capture a trace once (manually or via
`CommandRecorder`), then replay it as a regression test. A widget is "done" when all its
mutating actions produce golden traces that replay successfully.

---

## Definition of Done

A widget is fully command-ified when all three criteria are met:

1. **Zero direct `DataManager` mutations** — every `setData()`, `deleteData()`,
   `getData<T>()->addEvent()`, etc. in the widget goes through `createCommand()` +
   `execute()`. Category C verbs (TransformsV2, LoaderRegistry) are documented but
   intentionally left as direct calls until Phase 3+.

2. **Zero MutationGuard warnings** — exercising the widget in a debug build produces no
   `"DataManager mutation outside command"` warnings in the console.

3. **Golden trace test exists** — at least one Catch2 test loads a recorded command sequence
   (JSON fixture), replays it against synthetic data, and verifies the final
   `DataManagerSnapshot` matches expectations.

Criteria 2 and 3 require Steps 1.13–1.14 to be implemented first.

---

## Phase 2 — Triage Session

**Goal:** Mark/Commit/Recall workflow using command sequences.

**Depends on:** Phase 1

### Step 2.1 — TriageSession Core Class

- [x] `TriageSession` non-Qt class in `src/TriageSession/` (separate static library, not inside DataManager)
- [x] State machine: `Idle` ↔ `Marking` transitions via `mark()`, `commit()`, `recall()`
- [x] `commit()` populates `CommandContext::runtime_variables` with `mark_frame` and `current_frame`, delegates to `executeSequence()`
- [x] Pipeline configuration: `setPipeline()` / `pipeline()` accessors
- [x] `lastCommitCommands()` / `takeLastCommitCommands()` for undo support
- [x] Enhanced `substituteVariables()` to handle numeric type coercion for `"${var}"` patterns

See [Triage Session](index.qmd#triage-session) in the design document.

### Step 2.2 — TriageSessionState (EditorState)

- [x] `TriageSessionState` EditorState subclass in `src/WhiskerToolbox/TriageSession_Widget/Core/`
- [x] Serializes pipeline JSON and tracked-regions key
- [x] State machine resets to Idle on workspace load (never resume mid-triage)
- [x] Signals: `pipelineChanged()`, `stateChanged()`

### Step 2.3 — TriageSessionWidget (Qt UI)

- [x] Mark / Commit / Recall buttons with status display
- [x] Pipeline display: load JSON, edit JSON, show command summary
- [x] Tracked-regions summary: interval count, frame count

See [UI Sketch](index.qmd#ui-sketch) in the design document.

### Step 2.4 — Keyboard Shortcuts

- [ ] **M** — Mark (start a triage range)
- [ ] **C** — Commit (execute pipeline for `[mark_frame, current_frame]`)
- [ ] **R** — Recall (abort, jump back to mark frame)

### Step 2.5 — Tracked-Regions Visualization

- [ ] Render tracked-regions `DigitalIntervalSeries` as shaded overlay on the timeline/scrubber

### Step 2.6 — Unit Tests

- [x] State machine transitions: Idle → Marking → Idle (commit), Marking → Idle (recall)
- [x] Commit populates correct runtime variables
- [x] Recall resets state without executing commands
- [x] Integration test: full triage round-trip with mock DataManager (mark → scrub → commit → verify data moved + interval added)

### Step 2.7 — Extract AutoParamWidget Library

- [x] Create `src/WhiskerToolbox/AutoParamWidget/` as a new static library
- [x] Move `AutoParamWidget.hpp/.cpp` out of `src/WhiskerToolbox/TransformsV2_Widget/UI/`
- [x] Library depends on `Qt6::Widgets` and `ParameterSchema` — no TransformsV2 dependency
- [x] Update `TransformsV2_Widget` CMakeLists to depend on `AutoParamWidget` and remove moved sources
- [x] Update all TransformsV2_Widget includes to reference the new location
- [x] Verify existing TransformsV2_Widget functionality is unaffected

### Step 2.8 — Guided Pipeline Editor

- [x] Add "Add Command..." button to triage pipeline section
- [x] Implement command picker dialog/dropdown populated by `getAvailableCommands()`
- [x] Generate `AutoParamWidget` from selected command's `ParameterSchema`
- [x] Replace read-only command summary with an editable command list (each row is an expandable `AutoParamWidget` with current parameter values)
- [x] Edits to command rows update the `CommandSequenceDescriptor` and raw JSON view
- [x] Keep raw JSON editor as a collapsible section for power users
- [x] Handle `${variable}` references in text fields (accept both literal values and variable strings)

---

## Phase 3 — Persistence Commands & Save Registry

**Goal:** Unified `SaveData` command wrapping all existing saver functions.

**Depends on:** Phase 1

### Step 3.1.1 — SaverRegistry Core Infrastructure

- [x] `SaverInfo` metadata struct (`format`, `data_type`, `description`, `ParameterSchema`) in `src/DataManager/IO/core/SaverInfo.hpp`
- [x] `getSupportedSaveFormats()` query on `LoaderRegistry`; per-loader `getSaverInfo()` virtual method
- [x] `AtomicWrite` utility (`atomicWriteFile()`) in `src/DataManager/IO/core/AtomicWrite.hpp/.cpp` — write to `.tmp`, `fsync()`, rename over target
- [x] Saver return types audited — all savers updated to return `bool`

See [SaverInfo & AtomicWrite](../../IO/saver_infrastructure.qmd) for infrastructure details.

### Step 3.1.2 — CSV PointData Saver

- [x] `save(PointData const*, CSVPointSaverOptions)` returns `bool`, uses `atomicWriteFile()`
- [x] Registered in `CSVLoader::getSaverInfo()`
- [x] `ParameterUIHints<CSVPointSaverOptions>` tooltips added
- [x] Round-trip fuzz tests (`FuzzCsvPointRoundTrip`, `FuzzCsvPointRoundTripDelimiter`)
- [x] Deterministic unit tests (single point, multiple points, empty data, negative coords, space-delimited)
- [x] Sources migrated from `Points/IO/CSV/` → `IO/formats/CSV/points/`

### Step 3.1.3 — CSV LineData Saver (Single-File and Multi-File)

- [x] Both `save()` overloads (`CSVSingleFileLineSaverOptions`, `CSVMultiFileLineSaverOptions`) return `bool`, use `atomicWriteFile()`
- [x] Single-file variant registered in `CSVLoader::getSaverInfo()`
- [x] `ParameterUIHints` added for both option structs
- [x] Round-trip fuzz tests for both variants
- [x] Deterministic unit tests for both variants
- [x] Sources migrated from `Lines/IO/CSV/` → `IO/formats/CSV/lines/`

### Step 3.1.4 — CSV AnalogTimeSeries Saver

- [x] `save(AnalogTimeSeries const*, CSVAnalogSaverOptions const&)` returns `bool`, uses `atomicWriteFile()`
- [x] Registered in `CSVLoader::getSaverInfo()`
- [x] `ParameterUIHints<CSVAnalogSaverOptions>` tooltips added
- [x] Round-trip fuzz tests (`FuzzCsvAnalogRoundTrip`, `FuzzCsvAnalogRoundTripDelimiter`)
- [x] Deterministic unit tests (single sample, multiple samples, negative values, space-delimited)

### Step 3.1.5 — CSV DigitalEventSeries Saver

- [x] `save(DigitalEventSeries const*, CSVEventSaverOptions const&)` returns `bool`, uses `atomicWriteFile()`
- [x] Registered in `CSVLoader::getSaverInfo()`
- [x] `ParameterUIHints<CSVEventSaverOptions>` tooltips added
- [x] Round-trip fuzz tests (`FuzzCsvEventRoundTrip`, `FuzzCsvEventRoundTripDelimiter`)
- [x] Deterministic unit tests (single event, multiple events, empty data, large times, space-delimited)

### Step 3.1.6 — CSV DigitalIntervalSeries Saver

- [x] `save(DigitalIntervalSeries const*, CSVIntervalSaverOptions const&)` returns `bool`, uses `atomicWriteFile()`
- [x] Registered in `CSVLoader::getSaverInfo()`
- [x] `ParameterUIHints<CSVIntervalSaverOptions>` tooltips added
- [x] Round-trip fuzz tests (`FuzzCsvIntervalRoundTrip`, `FuzzCsvIntervalRoundTripDelimiter`)
- [x] Existing unit tests updated to check `bool` return

### Step 3.1.7 — CSV MaskData (RLE) Saver

- [x] `save(MaskData const*, CSVMaskRLESaverOptions const&)` returns `bool`, uses `atomicWriteFile()`
- [x] Registered in `CSVLoader::getSaverInfo()`
- [x] `ParameterUIHints<CSVMaskRLESaverOptions>` tooltips added
- [x] Round-trip fuzz tests (`FuzzCsvMaskRleRoundTrip`, `FuzzCsvMaskRleRoundTripDelimiter`)
- [x] Deterministic unit tests (single mask, multiple frames, empty data, large coords, no-header, CSV contents)

### Step 3.1.8 — CapnProto LineData Saver

- [x] `save(LineData const&, BinaryLineSaverOptions const&)` uses `atomicWriteFile()`; options const-corrected
- [x] `getSaverInfo()` override on `CapnProtoFormatLoader` for `IODataType::Line`
- [x] `ParameterUIHints<BinaryLineSaverOptions>` tooltips added
- [x] Round-trip fuzz test (`FuzzCapnProtoLineRoundTrip`) — exact match (binary format, no FP loss)
- [x] Deterministic unit tests (single line, multiple lines, multiple frames, negative coords, image size, save-returns-true)

### Step 3.1.9 — OpenCV MaskData (Image) Saver

- [x] `save(MaskData const*, ImageMaskSaverOptions const&)` returns `bool`; per-image atomic write via temp-prefixed `cv::imwrite()` + rename
- [x] `getSaverInfo()` override on `OpenCVFormatLoader` for `IODataType::Mask`
- [x] `ParameterUIHints<ImageMaskSaverOptions>` tooltips added
- [x] Robustness fuzz tests (`FuzzOpenCVMaskSave`, `FuzzOpenCVMaskSaveFormats`) — no crash, valid files produced
- [x] Deterministic unit tests (single mask, multiple frames, empty data, null pointer, custom prefix/padding, overwrite protection, BMP format)

### Step 3.2 — SaveData Command

- [x] `SaveDataParams` struct: `data_key`, `format`, `path`, optional `format_options`
- [x] `SaveData` dispatches via `LoaderRegistry::trySave()`, not undoable
- [x] Registered in factory with `category = "persistence"`
- [x] `ParameterUIHints<SaveDataParams>` tooltips; `format_options` marked `is_advanced`
- [x] 11 unit tests (factory, execution, serialization, integration)
- [x] Developer documentation: [SaveData Command](save_data.qmd)

See [SaveData](index.qmd#savedata) and [Persistence Safety](index.qmd#persistence-safety) in the design document.

### Step 3.3 — LoadData Command

- [x] `LoadDataParams` struct: `data_key`, `data_type`, `filepath`, `format`, optional `format_options`
- [x] `LoadData` dispatches via `LoaderRegistry::tryLoad()`, stores result in `DataManager`; undoable (`undo()` calls `deleteData()`)
- [x] Registered in factory with `category = "persistence"`, `supports_undo = true`
- [x] `ParameterUIHints<LoadDataParams>` tooltips; `format_options` marked `is_advanced`
- [x] 11 unit tests (factory, execution including undo, serialization, Load+Save round-trip)
- [x] Developer documentation: [LoadData Command](load_data.qmd)

---

## Phase 4 — Action Journal Integration

**Goal:** Commands auto-record to journal; journal can replay command entries.

**Depends on:** Phase 1 (Step 1.13 CommandRecorder), State Management Roadmap Phase 4 (Action Journal)

**Relationship to Step 1.13:** The `CommandRecorder` from Step 1.13 is the minimal precursor
to the full Action Journal. Phase 4 generalizes the recorder into a persistent, timestamped,
filterable log with workspace integration. The `executeSequence()` hook added in Step 1.13
is reused here — the only change is swapping the `CommandRecorder*` parameter for an
`ActionJournal*` that wraps it with persistence and metadata.

### Step 4.1 — Journal Hook in executeSequence

- [ ] Generalize the `CommandRecorder*` parameter from Step 1.13 into `ActionJournal*`
- [ ] On successful command execution, record `ActionEntry` with timestamp, command name/description, serialized parameters, source widget

### Step 4.2 — Journal Replay

- [ ] Deserialize `ActionEntry::payload_json` via `createCommand()` + `execute()` for replay
- [ ] Journal export: filter out undone entries, produce clean `CommandSequenceDescriptor` JSON

### Step 4.3 — WorkspaceManager Integration

- [ ] Connect journal recording to existing `WorkspaceManager` provenance tracking

---

## Phase 5 — Undo/Redo

**Goal:** CommandStack, EditorState memento undo, inverse-command undo for data mutations.

**Depends on:** Phase 1

### Step 5.1 — CommandStack

- [ ] `CommandStack` class: push executed commands, undo/redo with `CommandContext`
- [ ] Clean state tracking: `markClean()` / `isClean()` for save-state awareness
- [ ] Redo stack cleared on new command push

See [CommandStack](index.qmd#commandstack) in the design document.

### Step 5.2 — WidgetStateChangeCommand

- [ ] Generic memento-based command for any `EditorState`
- [ ] Captures before/after JSON snapshots
- [ ] `mergeWith()` for coalescing rapid changes to the same widget

See [WidgetStateChangeCommand](index.qmd#widgetstatechangecommand) in the design document.

### Step 5.3 — CommandSequenceCommand

- [ ] Wraps `vector<unique_ptr<ICommand>>` for macro undo (e.g., triage commits)
- [ ] `isUndoable()` returns true if any child is undoable
- [ ] `undo()` reverses children in reverse order, skipping non-undoable ones

See [CommandSequenceCommand](index.qmd#commandsequencecommand-triage-undo) in the design document.

### Step 5.4 — MoveByTimeRange Undo

- [ ] Verify `MoveByTimeRange` correctly captures entity IDs during `execute()` and moves them back during `undo()`
- [ ] Integration test: move → undo → verify original state restored

### Step 5.5 — UI Integration

- [ ] Edit → Undo / Redo menu items
- [ ] Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Shift+Z keyboard shortcuts
- [ ] Integration with dirty tracking (`EditorState::isDirty()`, `WorkspaceManager`)

---

## Phase 6 — CLI Batch Executor & Macro Recording

**Goal:** Execute command sequences headlessly; record GUI actions as commands.

**Depends on:** Phases 1 (Steps 1.13–1.14), 3, 4

**Relationship to Steps 1.13–1.14:** The CLI executor is a direct generalization of the golden
trace test pattern (Step 1.14) — same infrastructure (load JSON, create `DataManager`,
`executeSequence()`, check results), different entry point (CLI instead of Catch2). The macro
recorder generalizes `CommandRecorder` (Step 1.13) from a test utility into a user-facing
feature.

### Step 6.1 — CLI Executor

- [ ] CLI tool or application mode: load a `.json` command sequence, create `DataManager`, execute, exit
- [ ] Variable substitution for batch parameterization (reuses existing pipeline variable system)
- [ ] Exit code reflects success/failure

### Step 6.2 — Macro Recorder

- [ ] Intercept GUI actions (data loads, transforms, triage commits) and emit `CommandDescriptor`s
- [ ] Record to an in-memory `CommandSequenceDescriptor` that can be saved to JSON

### Step 6.3 — ConfigExportToolbar

- [ ] Reusable toolbar component: "Copy as JSON", "Save Configuration..."
- [ ] Attach to widgets that produce command sequences (triage session, transform widget, etc.)

### Step 6.4 — CommandSequenceWidget

- [ ] Standalone Qt widget (`src/WhiskerToolbox/Commands/`) for building, editing, and running
  command sequences outside the triage workflow
- [ ] JSON editor with validation feedback
- [ ] Command list view with run button

---

## Relationship to State Management Roadmap

This roadmap replaces Phases 4–6 of the
[State Management Roadmap](../../STATE_MANAGEMENT_ROADMAP.md) with a more comprehensive
command-first architecture. The key differences:

| State Mgmt Roadmap (old) | Command Architecture (this doc) |
|---|---|
| Phase 4: Action Journal records ad-hoc action types | Phase 4 here: Action Journal records `CommandDescriptor`s — standardized, replayable |
| Phase 5: `Command` interface with `execute()`/`undo()` as standalone pattern | Phases 1+5 here: `ICommand` virtual base with factory, `CommandStack` layered on top |
| Phase 6: `SessionReplayer` replays workspace provenance | Phase 6 here: CLI executor replays `CommandSequenceDescriptor` — same mechanism as triage |
| No concept of triage or batch operations | Triage Session is Phase 2; demonstrates the architecture's power |
| `JSONConsole_Widget` dispatches by type | JSON Console dispatches to `executeSequence()` for command sequences |

Ideas **carried forward** from the State Management Roadmap (not lost):

- **Action Journal** (`ActionEntry`, append-only log, JSONL format, query/filter) — Phase 4 here
- **JSON Console Widget** (`JSONEditor` component, schema validation, multi-type dispatch) — integrated via JSON Console Integration section
- **CommandStack** (undo/redo, clean state, merge) — Phase 5 here
- **Memento-based EditorState undo** (`WidgetStateChangeCommand`) — Phase 5
- **Command merging** for sequential similar operations — Phase 5 (built into `ICommand::mergeWith()`)
- **Journal compaction** (filter undone entries on save) — Phase 4
- **ConfigExportToolbar** ("Copy as JSON", "Send to Console", "Save Configuration...") — Phase 6
- **Session replay / batch processing** via parameterized journal replay — Phase 6
- **Incremental adoption** (not every operation needs to be a command immediately) — design principle

The State Management Roadmap should be updated to reference this document for Phases 4–6 and
remove the detailed designs that now live here.

## See Also

- [Command Architecture Design Document](index.qmd) — full architecture, types, and code examples
- [Data Manager](../data_manager.qmd) — core data management documentation
